title: chelidonium leptopodum
intro: Chelidonium leptopodum, also known as celandine, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the poppy family (Papaveraceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve liver and gallbladder issues, and act as an anti-inflammatory agent. The health benefits of celandine include its potential to protect the liver from toxins, reduce inflammation and pain, and support the body's natural detoxification processes. Celandine can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and ointments, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including skin issues and digestive problems.
